essence: Restores public and private spaces by safely removing graffiti, markings, and surface damage.

identity.title: Graffiti Removal Technician
identity.summary: Uses specialist equipment and cleaning methods to remove graffiti from buildings, walls, transport infrastructure, and public spaces.

salary.notes: Specialists in heritage surfaces, chemical treatments, or rapid‑response teams can exceed £35k-£40k+.

qualifications: Training in safe chemical handling and COSHH awareness
Knowledge of pressure‑washing and surface‑cleaning techniques
Understanding of surface types (brick, stone, metal, paintwork)
Ability to work outdoors and at height when required

lifestyle.description: Graffiti removal technicians travel between sites cleaning graffiti from buildings, walls, transport hubs, and public spaces. The role involves using pressure washers, chemical treatments, and protective coatings. It suits individuals who enjoy practical work, visible results, and contributing to clean, safe communities.

habits: Assesses surfaces before choosing cleaning methods
Follows strict safety and chemical‑handling procedures
Documents completed work with photos and notes
Communicates clearly with supervisors and property owners
